name: Amelia Ascione
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Mars remote sensing • Thermal inertia mapping • Surface process analysis
bio: Amelia Ascione is a Ph.D. student in Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University, working in the Planetary Instrumentation eXperimentation and Exploration Laboratory (PIXEL). Her research focuses on planetary surface processes and remote sensing, with a particular emphasis on Mars. She uses thermal infrared data from instruments such as THEMIS to generate maps of rock abundance and thermal inertia, helping to better understand surface composition and geologic activity on the planet. Her work also includes analyzing high-resolution imagery to study features such as landslides and slope processes, and she has contributed to published research on Martian surface dynamics. By integrating remote sensing data, geospatial analysis, and planetary geology, Ascione contributes to advancing knowledge of how planetary surfaces evolve over time.
